beats.narration_text: Merhaba. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of finite square well explorer. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of finite square well explorer

beats.narration_text: Finite Square Well Explorer is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. a = 0.5 nm · V₀ slider 0 – 100 eV. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Finite Square Well Explorer

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Finite Square Well Explorer. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: sliderV0. Verify: a = 0.5 nm · V₀ slider 0 – 100 eV. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Finite Square Well Explorer. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: sliderV0. Verify: a = 0.5 nm · V₀ slider 0

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Finite Square Well Explorer — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from qmcg-finite-well-explorer.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: finite square well explorer. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 0.05 to 100. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0.05 to 100

beats.narration_text: a = 0.5 nm · V₀ slider 0 – 100 eV. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of finite square well explorer. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Finite Square Well Explorer simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Finite Square Well Explorer simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3

beats.narration_text: Finite Square Well Explorer — 1D vs 3D. Medhavy.
beats.new_visual_element: Outro: Finite Square Well Explorer — 1D vs 3D · @Medhavy
